prompt: Write the opening paragraph of a children's book about a shy octopus who wants to make friends.
response: Deep in the sparkly blue depths of the ocean, where dappled sunlight danced across the coral towers, lived an eight-legged mite of anxiety named Oliver. This shy octopus with the world's most perfect oversized eyes and a habit of camouflaging himself to match any surrounding shade of shy, longed more than anything to join the fish of the sea. But every time he wiggled an inkling of a tentacle toward potential companions, his eight arms seemed to tie themselves in knots of uncertainty, and he'd vanish into a nearby rock like disappearing ink!
